content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base 'skeleton' that can be structurally augmented by multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hierarchical self-improvement system, where outer-loop iterative self-feedback is enriched by inner-contrastive multi-perspective reflection at each refinement step. This hybrid system retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data, while addressing the common limitation of single-perspective blind spots that plague standard iterative self-refinement.
